title: Bipolar & Lithium Management
content:
• Dx: Manic Episode: Sleeping little for 8 days, pressured speech, risky behavior. Bipolar I if cycled with depression.
• Risk: Lithium Toxicity: Precipitated by NSAIDs (Advil). Use Aspirin or Sulindac for pain.
• Symptoms: N/V/D, coarse tremor, ataxia, confusion, slurred speech.
• Dx: EKG findings: T-wave flattening or inversion + U waves.
• Tx: Fluid resuscitation; Emergent dialysis if Level >4 or kidney disease.
• Monitoring: Li level q4-8wks, TFTs q6mo, Cr.

title: Personality Disorder Clusters
content:
• Cluster A (Weird): Paranoid (Accusatory/Distrust), Schizoid (Aloof/Detached), Schizotypal (Awkward/Magical thinking).
• Cluster B (Wild): Antisocial (Bad/Criminal), Borderline (Instability/Splitting), Histrionic (flamBoyant/Seductive), Narcissistic (Best self/Grandiosity).
• Cluster C (Worried): Avoidant (Cowardly/Anxious), OCPD (Compulsive/Orderly), Dependent (Clingy).

title: Childhood Tics & Separation Anxiety
content:
• Dx: Tourette’s: Tics at least once a day for 1 year without a gap >3 months. Look for OCD comorbidity.
• Tx: First line? Clonidine (benign). Most effective? Haloperidol or Pimozide.
• Dx: Separation Anxiety: Abdominal pain only on school days, not weekends/summer.
• Dx: Reactive Attachment: Inhibited, hypervigilant, adopted child with poor relationship.
• Dx: Rumination Disorder: Regurgitating food. Risk: Check lead levels.

title: APGAR & Neonatal Injuries
content:
• Dx: APGAR: Tells labor tolerance (1m) & resuscitation response (5m). Does NOT predict neuro outcome.
• Dx: Erb-Duchenne (C5-C6): Right arm medially rotated (Waiters tip). Refer if not better by 3-6mo.
• Dx: Caput Succedaneum: Edema that crosses suture lines.
• Dx: Cephalohematoma: Fluctuance that does NOT cross suture lines.
• Tx: Clavicular Fracture: No tx needed; will form callus in 1 week.

title: Congenital Heart Defects
content:
• Dx: VSD: Most common defect. Harsh holosystolic murmur at LLSB.
• Dx: PDA: Continuous machine-like murmur. Tx: Indomethacin to close.
• Dx: TGA: Common in infants of diabetic mothers. Cyanotic at birth, O2 does not help.
• Dx: TOF: Cyanotic during play, squatting helps. SEM murmur + single S2.
• Dx: Ebstein's Anomaly: Associated with maternal Lithium use.

title: Common Pediatric GI Conditions
content:
• Dx: Pyloric Stenosis: 4-week-old, projectile non-bilious vomiting, olive mass. Tx: Hydration + Pyloromyotomy.
• Dx: Intussusception: Colicky pain, currant jelly stool, sausage mass. Tx: Air/Barium enema.
• Dx: Duodenal Atresia: Associated with Down Syndrome; Double-bubble sign on X-ray.

title: Consent & Capacity Mastery
content:
• Dx: Capacity Assessment: Check understanding, appreciation, reasoning, and choice.
• Risk: Adult Refusal: Do not override a capable adult even if life-saving (e.g., Jehovah’s Witness).
• Rule: Mature Minor: Consent is capacity-based, not age-based. Contraception/Abortion confidential.

title: Mandatory Reporting & Conflicts
content:
• Risk: Child Abuse: Report immediately to child protection on suspicion; do not investigate alone.
• Risk: Fitness to Drive: Counsel to stop and report per provincial mandate (e.g., seizures).
• Rule: Error Disclosure: Disclose promptly even if no harm occurred; apologize and document.
• Rule: Media requests: Decline patient-specific info; de-identify thoroughly if speaking.

title: Confidentiality Exceptions
content:
• Exceptions: Imminent risk to self/others, child protection, public health diseases, court order.
• Rule: Prisoners: Ensure privacy; ask guards to step out unless safety risk.
• Rule: Family requests: Do not omit facts if family asks 'don't chart this'; document accurately.
